title: quesnelia distichanta
intro: Quesnelia distichanta, also known as the rock rosette, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a terrestrial bromeliad that is native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in Brazil and Argentina. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been found to have potential health benefits including wound healing and anti-cancer properties. Quesnelia distichanta can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions, and has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
